items.title: CoRE: Concept-Reasoning Expansion for Continual Brain Lesion Segmentation
items.abstract: The CoRE framework addresses continual brain lesion segmentation by integrating visual features with structured concepts through a joint decision-making mechanism. It aligns image tokens with a hierarchical concept library to simulate clinical reasoning, guiding both interpretable expert routing and demand-based model expansion. This approach aims to overcome limitations in existing continual learning methods by incorporating explicit conceptual knowledge to handle pathological and multimodal heterogeneity.
items.figure_caption: Illustration of CL paradigms. (a-b) Traditional paradigm (left) : Existing methods typically (a) employ a fixed expert pool shared by all tasks or (b) add task-specific experts, which often suffer from limited capacity or linear parameter growth. (c-d) Self-expansion paradigm (right) : (c) Existing dynamic expansion methods rely solely on image perception strategies and visual shifts. (d) Our method incorporates brain lesion concepts to simulate the clinical cognitive process, guiding expert routing and architectural growth through stable concept reasoning.

items.title: Health System Scale Semantic Search Across Unstructured Clinical Notes
items.abstract: This paper details the engineering and deployment of a semantic search system capable of indexing and querying 166 million unstructured clinical notes across a large health system. The core method involves using instruction-tuned embeddings, optimized chunking, and a hybrid storage architecture (vector database and key-value store) to manage scale efficiently. The contribution is demonstrating the feasibility, performance, and governance compliance of deploying large-scale, concept-based clinical information retrieval within a real-world, HIPAA-compliant environment.

items.title: Robustness Evaluation of a Foundation Segmentation Model Under Simulated Domain Shifts in Abdominal CT: Implications for Health Digital Twin Deployment
items.abstract: This paper systematically evaluates the robustness of the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for spleen segmentation in abdominal CT against clinically relevant domain shifts. The core method involves applying controlled perturbations (noise, blur, contrast changes, etc.) to CT slices and measuring the resulting change in segmentation performance (Dice score). The key contribution is demonstrating that SAM exhibits high robustness, with minimal performance degradation ($\Delta$Dice $< 0.01$) under simulated inter-scanner variability, suggesting promise for its deployment in health digital twins.

items.title: Exploring Remote Photoplethysmography for Neonatal Pain Detection from Facial Videos
items.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, non-contact method for neonatal pain detection using remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) signals extracted from facial videos. To improve signal quality, the method incorporates a quality parameter to select temporal signals from facial regions least affected by skin deformations. The core contribution is demonstrating the feasibility of using these refined rPPG signals as objective physiological indicators for assessing pain in neonates.
items.figure_caption: The flow diagram of our proposed method. The input video is divided into multiple clips, the face region is detected and divided into ROIs, and temporal signals with higher quality are selected. The selected signals are used to extract the rPPG signal along with the fitness parameter. Finally, the rPPG signal with the highest fitness parameter is fed to the machine learning classifier for pain and no pain classification.

items.title: Localsend: An open-source cross-platform alternative to AirDrop
items.summary: Localsend is an open-source, cross-platform application designed to offer a local file-sharing alternative similar to Apple's AirDrop.

items.title: VibeVoice: Open-source frontier voice AI
items.summary: Microsoft has released VibeVoice, an open-source project focused on advancing frontier voice AI technology.

items.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the API
items.summary: Claude.ai experienced an outage with elevated errors reported on its API, rendering the service temporarily unavailable.

items.title: Google and Pentagon reportedly agree on deal for 'any lawful' use of AI
items.summary: Google and the Pentagon have reportedly reached an agreement allowing the military to use Google's AI technology for any lawful purpose.

items.title: AI's economics don't make sense
items.summary: The core issue is that the current economics of AI development and deployment are unsustainable, as the costs of training and running models often outweigh the potential revenue or value they generate.

items.title: AISLE Discovers 38 CVEs in OpenEMR Healthcare Software
items.summary: AISLE researchers discovered 38 critical security vulnerabilities (CVEs) within the widely used OpenEMR healthcare software.

items.title: Show HN: Live Sun and Moon Dashboard with NASA Footage
items.summary: A new web application, "Live Sun and Moon Dashboard," offers users real-time visualizations of the sun and moon, enhanced with actual footage sourced from NASA.

items.description: Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
items.summary: This GitHub repository, "skills," provides a collection of practical skills and knowledge curated by the author, likely derived from their personal development resources, aimed at real-world engineering applications.

items.description: GitNexus: The Zero-Server Code Intelligence Engine - GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs entirely in your browser. Drop in a GitHub repo or ZIP file, and get an interactive knowledge graph wit a built in Graph RAG Agent. Perfect for code exploration
items.summary: GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs in the browser, allowing users to generate interactive code knowledge graphs from GitHub repositories or ZIP files, complete with a built-in Graph RAG Agent for code exploration.

items.description: A curated list of practical Codex skills for automating workflows across the Codex CLI and API.
items.summary: This repository curates practical Codex skills for automating workflows, offering a valuable resource for leveraging the Codex CLI and API effectively.
VibeVoice is an open-source project from Microsoft focused on advancing the frontier of voice AI, likely encompassing technologies for realistic speech synthesis, recognition, or understanding.

items.description: CLI tool for configuring and monitoring Claude Code
items.summary: This CLI tool, `claude-code-templates`, provides users with essential functionality for configuring and monitoring Claude Code environments directly from the command line.

items.description: Useful tool to track location or mobile number
items.summary: GhostTrack is a useful tool designed to track the location or mobile number of a target.

items.description: 🎵 The Ultimate Open Source Suno Alternative - Professional UI for ACE-Step 1.5 AI Music Generation. Free, local, unlimited. Stop paying for Suno!
items.summary: This repository provides a professional, open-source user interface for the ACE-Step 1.5 AI music generation model, offering a free, local, and unlimited alternative to Suno.

items.description: A collective list of free APIs
items.summary: The **public-apis** repository curates a comprehensive, community-driven list of free Application Programming Interfaces across various categories.

items.description: Deepseek to API: A lightweight, high-performance full-stack middleware converting client protocols to universal APIs. Supports multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel Serverless, and Docker. Compatible with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.
items.summary: ds2api is a lightweight, high-performance middleware that converts client protocols to universal APIs for Deepseek, featuring multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel/Docker deployment, and compatibility with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.
